bibliographic_data.title: How University Disability Services Professionals Write Image Descriptions for HCI Figures Using Generative AI
bibliographic_data.abstract: Disability Services Office (DSO) professionals at higher education institutions write alt text for visual content, but struggle to produce high-quality descriptions for complex figures like HCI diagrams due to a lack of subject expertise. Generative AI has potential in this domain, yet its support for DSO professionals is underexplored and the quality of AI-generated alt text remains untested. This work investigates the use of generative AI by DSO professionals and evaluates the resulting alt text with input from HCI experts. Findings show that AI-assisted descriptions are more accurate and helpful than those written solely by DSO professionals, but also highlight inefficiencies in interactions between humans and AI.

core_content.novelty_claim: This work explores the use of generative AI to assist DSO professionals in writing alt text for complex HCI figures, demonstrating that AI-assisted descriptions are more accurate and helpful than those written solely by DSO professionals.
core_content.key_methodology: The study involved two phases: first, 12 DSO professionals wrote alt text for HCI figures with and without the assistance of generative AI. Second, 11 HCI experts evaluated the quality of these alt texts. The findings highlight the benefits and limitations of using AI in this context.
outcomes: DSO professionals writing alt text solely produced lower-quality descriptions than those assisted by AI.

AI-assisted descriptions were more accurate and helped DSO professionals write alt text more quickly and with greater confidence, but also highlighted inefficiencies in interactions between humans and AI.
core_content.full_text_markdown: This paper investigates the challenges faced by Disability Services Office (DSO) professionals when writing alt text for complex figures like HCI diagrams. Due to a lack of subject expertise, DSO professionals often struggle to produce high-quality descriptions. Generative artificial intelligence (AI) has potential in this domain but its support for DSO professionals is underexplored and the quality of AI-generated alt text remains untested. The study involved two phases: first, 12 DSO professionals wrote alt text for HCI figures with and without the assistance of generative AI. Second, 11 HCI experts evaluated the quality of these alt texts. Findings show that AI-assisted descriptions are more accurate and helpful than those written solely by DSO professionals, but also highlight inefficiencies in interactions between humans and AI.
